text: Academic publishing today presents a multitude of challenges in a digital era; particularly with respect to scholarly communication and access to scholarly literature. Through examination of the barriers that have been imposed with the move from print to closed access on-line platforms, this paper investigates obstacles to the unencumbered dissemination of scientific information. It finds that present-day licensing restrictions severely hinder the dissemination of the most critical research, creating a disconnect between information availability and researcher requirements. This has motivated the work here proposing a more holistic approach to open access publishing practices with a specific recommendation to take measures to better align institutional access points so as to facilitate ongoing communicative interaction within the scientific community.
